name: Keep the current best branch and raise the hot-band K16 loop to the next small unroll factor

summary.name: Keep the current best branch and raise the hot-band K16 loop to the next small unroll factor
summary.hypothesis: Round 27 confirmed that the lighter compiler approach to fixed-loop overhead works: restoring the best branch and moving away from `unroll 1` delivered a large gain without disturbing correctness. The next logical move is to stay on that axis and raise the K16 hot-band loop to the next small unroll factor. This keeps the same grouped-order base, the same `launch_bounds(128, 2)` clue, and the same control structure, while giving ptxas more room to schedule across adjacent iterations.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Residual loop-control and scheduling overhead in the current best hot-band K16 kernel.
summary.risk: Larger unroll factors can quickly increase register pressure or code size. The gain may flatten or reverse after the first successful step.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate on all benchmark cases
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the higher unroll factor regresses runtime or pushes register pressure up enough to undo the round-27 win.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
